questions.promptTex: \text{Translate } A(2, 5) \text{ by the vector } (3, -1). \text{ Find } A'.
hints: Add $d_x$ to the x-coordinate, $d_y$ to the y-coordinate.

questions.prompt: Which property is NOT preserved by a translation?
hints: Translations slide the shape; what changes?
Everything stays the same except where the shape sits.
questions.explanation: A translation changes only position. Side lengths, angles, and orientation are all preserved (translations are rigid motions).

questions.prompt: What is the inverse of the translation by (-4, 6)?
hints: If you go left 4 and up 6, what brings you back?
questions.explanation: The inverse of $(d_x, d_y)$ is $(-d_x, -d_y)$. So $(-4, 6)$ undone is $(4, -6)$.

steps.text: **From the x-coordinates** of pre-image and image:

steps.math: x' = x + d_x \;\Longrightarrow\; d_x = x' - x = 2 - (-3) = 5

questions.promptTex: \text{Triangle } \triangle ABC \text{ has vertices } A(1,2),\, B(4,2),\, C(3,5). \\ \text{After translating by } (-2, 3), \text{ what are the new coordinates of } C?
hints: Apply the rule $(x, y) \to (x - 2, y + 3)$ to $C$.

questions.promptTex: \text{If a translation sends } (0, 0) \to (4, -3), \\ \text{what is the magnitude (length) of the translation vector?}
\text{A translation sends } P(5, -2) \to P'(0, 4). \text{ The vector is:}

questions.prompt: Translation A: vector (3, 1). Translation B: vector (-2, 4). Apply A then B. What is the combined translation vector?
hints: Translations compose by adding their vectors.
questions.explanation: Composing two translations gives a single translation by the sum: $(3 + (-2),\; 1 + 4) = (1, 5)$.

steps.text: **Composing translations.** Doing translation A then translation B is the same as doing one translation by the sum of the two vectors.
